heroHeadline: RSF Drones Strike El-Obeid for Second Consecutive Day; Senior RSF Commander Defects to SAF After Death Sentence
heroSubtitle: An RSF drone struck residential neighborhoods in El-Obeid, North Kordofan on April 25, killing seven and wounding 22 — the second RSF drone attack on the city in two days, after April 24's strike killed 13 including 8 children. The Sudan Doctors Network condemned the April 25 attack as 'deliberate targeting of densely populated neighborhoods to cause the highest possible number of civilian casualties.' Meanwhile, Hemedti sentenced defected Major General Al-Nour Ahmed Adam ('Al-Qubba') to death in absentia on April 23 after the senior North Darfur RSF commander executed a dramatic desert escape to SAF-held Dongola — highlighting growing internal fractures within the RSF's Darfur command. The defection followed the RSF's October 2025 capture of El Fasher and bitter internal disputes over command appointments. These events unfold as Sudan enters Day 1107 of the civil war: 34 million people (65% of the population) require urgent humanitarian support, only 16% of the $3B 2026 UN appeal is funded, and near-daily RSF drone strikes continue to kill civilians in SAF-held Kordofan cities that already suffered 700+ drone deaths in Q1 2026.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle, Daily Pakistan, The Star (Reuters), AllAfrica, Soufan Center, AP, Canada Global Affairs, Middle East Monitor, Chatham House
